titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/152

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=425 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:15:18.270326+00 | translation moa=426 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:15:16.379082+00

div.p:
Omnibus Sancte matris Ecclesie filius tam presentibus quam futuris . Robertus de Bernaldebi . salutem . Sciatis me consensu et uoluntate heredum
meorum dedisse et concessisse et hac mea carta confirmasse deo et ecclesie Sancte Marie de Meylros et monachis . ibidem deo
seruientibus pro anima domini mei Asketini de ridale et omnium antecessorum et successorum suorum . et pro anima mea et omnium antecessorum et successorum meorum . uiginti . acras terre . scilicet rauenes fen . per diuisas quas ego et monachi de Meylros perambulauimus scilicet a capite superiori
de harehowedene totam illam leche sursum usque ad terram quam Willelmus de Ridale dedit matildi corbet uxori
sue in dotem . et inde sursum uersus wittun sicut sulcus trahitur inter predictam terram et rauenesfen usque ad foueam quam
monachi fecerunt . et inde intransuersum uersus harehov usque ad paruam spinam . et inde sicut fouee facte sunt inter terram
domini patricii et rauenesfen usque ad harecar . et inde sicut uetus fossatum decendit in harehowedene . Totam hanc terram
que infra predictas diuisas continetur cum omnibus pertinentiis suis dedi predicte ecclesie pro . uiginti . acris terre liberam solutam et quietam
ab omni terreno seruitio et consuetudine et exactione seculari . tenendam et possidendam . ita libere quiete . plenarie . et honor
ifice  sicut aliquam elemosinam liberius . quietius . plenius et honorificentius tenet et possidet . Quia ego et heredes mei
manutenebimus et warantizabimus monachis predictam terram contra omnes homines de omnibus rebus que pro terra exigi
possunt . Et ut hec mea elemosina firma sit et stabilis in perpetuum  dominus meus patricius de ridale eam mea petitione con
cessit et carta sua confirmauit . Et quia sigillum non habui rogaui dominum Simonem Glasguensem archidiaconum et Willelmum filium
Iohannis ut sigilla sua presenti carte apponerent . His Testibus domino Ioceelino Glasguensi episcopo . Ernaldo abbate Rieuall’ Simone
glasguensi archidiacono . Patricio de Ridale et Waltero filio eius . Willelmo filio Iohannis . Ricardo nano . Henrico de Molle . Simone
de Grubbeheued Adam de chathov . Adam de Wittun . Radulfo de Ridale

To all sons of Holy Mother Church as well present as to come, Robert of Barnoldby sends greeting. Know me by the consent and will of my heirs to have given and granted and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, for the soul of my lord Ansketil of Ryedale and of all his ancestors and successors, and for my own soul and of all my ancestors’ and successors’ souls, twenty acres of land, namely ‘Raven’s Fen’ by the boundaries which I and the monks of Melrose walked around, namely, from the upper head of ‘Harrowdean’, the whole sedge-bed, upwards as far as the land that William of Ryedale gave to Matilda Corbet his wife as a marriage-portion, and from there upwards towards Whitton as the ditch is drawn between the said land and ‘Raven’s Fen’, as far as the trench which the monks made, and from there across towards Harrow, as far as the small thornbush, and from there as the the trenches have been made between the land of the lord Patrick and ‘Raven’s Fen’ as far as ‘Harecar’, and from there as the old fosse descends to ‘Harrowdean’. This whole land which is comprised between the said boundaries with all its pertinents I have given to the said church in return for twenty acres of land free, loosed and quit from every earthly service and custom and secular exaction, to be held and possessed as freely, quitely, fully, and honourably as it holds and possesses any alms. For I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to the monks the said land against all men in respect of all things that can be exacted in return for land. And so that this my alms should be strong and established for ever, my lord Patrick of Ryedale at my request has licensed it and affirmed it by his charter. And since I do not have a seal, the lord Simon, archdeacon of Glasgow, and William son of John to apply their seals to the present charter. With these witnesses: the lord Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow; Arnold abbot of Rievaulx; Simon, archdeacon of Glasgow; Patrick of Ryedale and Walter his son; William son of John; Richard le Nain; Henry of Mow; Simon of Grubbit; Adam of Chatto; Adam of Whitton; Ralph of Ryedale.
